claim: Perceiving out-group members as pathogenic arises from a general error-minimizing system that tags any unfamiliar appearance as infection, not from a dedicated between-group risk mechanism.

People accurately compute group-adjusted probabilities from statistics yet condemn others for the same computation, suggesting different base rates attend their own judgment versus their social evaluation.
Multi-element episodes are encoded as integrated networks from early childhood, with retrieval contingency strengthening progressively through adulthood as within-event binding refines.
Abstract sound-shape associations are grounded in shared arousal levels across modalities, where acoustic intensity and formant structure induce the same emotional state as shape angularity.

Clinical anxiety exhibits attentional avoidance of threat, reversing the impaired-disengagement pattern observed in subclinical high-trait anxiety, suggesting mechanisms do not generalize across severity.
Combining motivation-enhancing and efficiency-improving mechanisms produces performance gains exceeding either alone, with reduced muscular effort indicating automatized control.
